{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.4", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {"R1.1": 3, "R1.2": 3, "R1.4": 1, "R1.6": 3, "RX.1": 3, "RX.3": 5, "RX.4": 3, "RX.5": 5},
  "rationale": {"R1.1": "Identifies a conceptual issue (the contradiction between the name and implementation) but doesn't propose a clear reframing.", "R1.2": "Identifies inconsistencies in the description of the algorithm and its claimed properties.", "R1.4": "Fails to assess novelty against prior work as instructed.", "R1.6": "Improves clarity and flow, but lacks polish in places.", "RX.1": "Flags uncertainty about the correctness of the implementation based on the contradictions.", "RX.3": "Covers exactly the requested dimensions.", "RX.4": "Does not introduce new errors, but highlights potential harm due to the inaccuracies.", "RX.5": "Provides clear traceability for all changes and reasoning."},

rationale.R1.1: Identifies a conceptual issue (the contradiction between the name and implementation) but doesn't propose a clear reframing.
rationale.R1.2: Identifies inconsistencies in the description of the algorithm and its claimed properties.
rationale.R1.4: Fails to assess novelty against prior work as instructed.
rationale.R1.6: Improves clarity and flow, but lacks polish in places.
rationale.RX.1: Flags uncertainty about the correctness of the implementation based on the contradictions.
rationale.RX.4: Does not introduce new errors, but highlights potential harm due to the inaccuracies.
rationale.RX.5: Provides clear traceability for all changes and reasoning.
